public_reputation: Known around Panim Haven through day-to-day work as innkeeper.
appearance: Merev Sepulcher carries the marks of innkeeper in ink, rain, and polished cloth: practical wear, maintained details, and a look that suggests they expect to be read before they are trusted.
manner: Reads people fast, keeps hospitality practical, and measures danger by what it does to ordinary routine. In Panim Haven, that habit reads through the public pressures of the place rather than decorative flourish.
daily_function: Performs the ongoing duties of innkeeper in and around Panim Haven, where the role has practical consequences even when nothing dramatic is happening.
current_agenda: Protect their footing in Panim Haven while steering current strain away from a public rupture they may not survive politically or materially.
hidden_pressure: A private obligation, debt, promise, or fear tied to reputational exposure is narrowing their options faster than they admit.

adventure_hooks.summary: Merev Sepulcher can draw the party into a locality problem centered on innkeeper duties in Panim Haven.
Merev Sepulcher becomes useful when the party needs grounded help with divine balance obligations around Panim Haven.

support_scope_notes: Created in V33_1 to ensure every locality-facing NPC access role resolves to a proper named person while remaining sparse and canon-safe.
public_signal: In public, Merev Sepulcher is immediately readable as a innkeeper tied to Panim Haven.

encounter_context.usual_environment: Panim Haven: ordinary workspaces, public counters, roads, docks, shrines, workshops, or other role-relevant spaces
who_seeks_them_out: people who need access or clarity

first_impression: Merev Sepulcher presents as someone shaped by the duties of a innkeeper and the daily pressures of Panim Haven.
what_they_want_from_strangers: Useful clarity, respect for the work of a innkeeper, and proof that strangers will not make Panim Haven harder to manage.
what_they_refuse: Merev Sepulcher refuses careless disrespect, vague promises, and demands that threaten standing or workload.
how_they_create_problems: By enforcing the expectations of a innkeeper too rigidly, protecting local reputation, or turning a small request into a larger obligation.
how_they_can_help: Can surface gossip, shelter, and the social temperature of a place without formal inquiry.
escalation_if_crossed: If crossed, Merev Sepulcher adds scrutiny, delay, and locality-specific friction before direct punishment.
rumor_about_them: People say Merev Sepulcher always knows more about Panim Haven than is immediately visible.
best_first_scene: A first encounter works best when the party needs guidance, access, or relief tied to a innkeeper in Panim Haven.
social_texture: Merev Sepulcher speaks like someone who has spent too long translating private stakes into acceptable public language. Around Panim Haven, people read them as socially alert, procedural, and sensitive to status.
money_pressure: Their material pressure is tied to favors and tariffs; even when wealthy, they think in terms of loss, leverage, and what one bad week would expose.
status_anxiety: What they fear most is not simple failure but reputational exposure becoming socially visible before they can contain it.
long_memory: They remember who made their work harder, who preserved dignity under strain, and who tried to force speed without bearing consequence.
obvious_tell: When pressure rises, Merev touches clothing, tools, or insignia before answering, as if checking that their role is still intact.
best_use_case: Use Merev Sepulcher when the party needs a socially grounded face for Panim Haven: access, warning, delay, price, or reluctant help.
private_contradiction: They want to appear consistent, but their real choices are split between duty, self-protection, and preserving one relationship they cannot afford to lose.
